notes_md:
**ngạc nhiên** = surprised, feeling something is unexpected.

It’s an adjective describing a person’s feeling, not the event itself. You use it after “be/feel” type verbs: *cảm thấy ngạc nhiên*, *rất ngạc nhiên*.  


Close synonym: **bất ngờ** (more like “unexpected” or “sudden surprise” — the event can be *bất ngờ*, but people are *ngạc nhiên*).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I was very surprised when you prepared a birthday party for me.
She was surprised to see the high score in the exam.
The whole class was surprised when the teacher arrived early today.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi thấy món quà.

translation.en: I felt surprised when I saw the gift.

example.en: I was surprised to see the gift.
example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy ngạc nhiên khi thấy món quà.

definitions.en: A feeling of astonishment or wonder.
definitions.es: Una sensación de asombro o maravilla.
definitions.vi: Cảm giác ngạc nhiên hoặc kinh ngạc.

translation.en: I am very surprised about the results.

exampleSentences: Anh rất ngạc nhiên khi em chuẩn bị bữa tiệc sinh nhật cho anh.
Chị đã ngạc nhiên khi nhìn thấy điểm số cao trong kỳ thi.
Cả lớp đều ngạc nhiên khi thầy giáo đến sớm hôm nay.

Close synonym: bất ngờ (more like “unexpected” or “sudden surprise” — the event can be bất ngờ, but people are ngạc nhiên).
